description: Apply the remaining SDK protocol consistency fixes by aligning world-state signing with the message domain, replacing ledger separator derivation with the canonical versioned constant, making the `/peer/message` callback path explicitly return `reply`, and verifying the scoped changes with targeted regression coverage plus full build and test validation.

userStories.title: Confirm scoped files and excluded prior fixes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the exact implementation scope confirmed so that the work stays limited to the three known SDK consistency fixes and does not reopen prior protocol fixes.
acceptanceCriteria: Implementation scope is limited to `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/world-server.ts`, `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/world-ledger.ts`, `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/peer-protocol.ts`, and minimal verification files if required.
Implementation notes identify the three target changes: world-state message-domain signing, canonical versioned ledger separator construction, and explicit Fastify reply return in `/peer/message`.
Implementation notes explicitly exclude rework of base58 leading-zero handling, world-state recipient restriction, and key-rotation binding.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Confirmed the scoped implementation surface for this branch is `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/world-server.ts`, `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/world-ledger.ts`, and `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/peer-protocol.ts`, with only minimal targeted regression files under `test/` if coverage is needed. The three intended changes are: switch `broadcastWorldState()` signing to `DOMAIN_SEPARATORS.MESSAGE`, define `LEDGER_SEPARATOR` directly as `AgentWorld-Ledger-${PROTOCOL_VERSION}\0`, and explicitly `return reply` after `reply.send()` in the callback-based `/peer/message` path. This scope explicitly excludes reopening the already-handled Base58 leading-zero codec work, world-state recipient restriction and endpoint ownership logic, and `/peer/key-rotation` binding validation.

userStories.title: Identify minimal regression coverage points
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the existing tests and verification gaps identified up front so that any new coverage stays minimal and targeted to the three scoped fixes.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing test coverage or verification gaps are identified for world-state signing, ledger separator construction, and `/peer/message` reply handling.
Any planned new or updated tests are limited to the three scoped fixes.
Coverage planning explicitly avoids duplicating prior regression tests for base58, recipient restriction, and key-rotation binding.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reviewed the existing coverage around the three scoped fixes. For world-state signing, `test/world-state-broadcast.test.mjs` currently verifies recipient restriction and endpoint ownership only, while `test/domain-separation.test.mjs` still asserts that a `world.state` signature created with `DOMAIN_SEPARATORS.WORLD_STATE` cannot verify as a regular message, so the message-domain change will require a focused expectation update or replacement there plus a narrow broadcast-oriented regression that stays out of recipient-selection logic. For the ledger separator, `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/world-ledger.ts` still derives `LEDGER_SEPARATOR` from `DOMAIN_SEPARATORS.MESSAGE`, and there is no direct test that asserts the exact canonical constant ``AgentWorld-Ledger-${PROTOCOL_VERSION}\0`` yet, so a minimal regression can live in a dedicated root `node:test` file that imports the built SDK artifact. For the `/peer/message` callback reply path, existing request-signing, transport-enforcement, and response-signing suites hit `/peer/message` over HTTP, but none exercise `registerPeerRoutes()` with a custom `onMessage` callback that calls `reply.send()`, so the narrowest follow-up is an SDK-level `fastify.inject()` regression alongside the existing direct route tests in `test/key-rotation-sdk.test.mjs` or a similarly focused SDK test file. This planning explicitly avoids duplicating the completed Base58, world-state recipient restriction, and key-rotation binding regressions.

userStories.title: Switch world-state signing to the message domain
userStories.description: As a developer, I need `broadcastWorldState()` to sign outbound `world.state` payloads with the standard message domain so that peer verification follows the same protocol path as other signed messages.
acceptanceCriteria: `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/world-server.ts` signs `world.state` message bodies with `DOMAIN_SEPARATORS.MESSAGE`.
`packages/agent-world-sdk/src/world-server.ts` no longer uses `DOMAIN_SEPARATORS.WORLD_STATE` for message-body signing in `broadcastWorldState()`.
Existing recipient-selection and endpoint-delivery logic in `broadcastWorldState()` remains otherwise unchanged.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Updated `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/world-server.ts` so `broadcastWorldState()` signs outbound `world.state` payloads with `DOMAIN_SEPARATORS.MESSAGE`, preserving the existing recipient and endpoint-delivery flow. Verified with `npm --prefix packages/agent-world-sdk run build`.

userStories.notes: Extended `test/world-state-broadcast.test.mjs` to validate the actual intercepted `world.state` messages emitted by `broadcastWorldState()`. The regression now verifies each broadcast signature against `DOMAIN_SEPARATORS.MESSAGE` and confirms the same payload does not verify under `DOMAIN_SEPARATORS.WORLD_STATE`, keeping the coverage focused on signing-domain behavior without reworking recipient-selection assertions.
